consistency.notes: The spell description is consistent with standard D&D 5e spell format. Level (5th), school (divination), ritual tag, and class availability (warlock, wizard) are standard entries.

quality.notes: The spell description is detailed and clearly outlines both the risk (insanity upon failure) and the reward (five one-word answers). The mechanical penalty for failure is severe (6d6 psychic damage and long-term insanity), which appropriately matches the high-level nature of contacting powerful entities.
quality.improvements_suggested: None apparent; the description is well-written and flavorful.

mechanics.casting_time: 1 minute (Long for a non-ritual spell, but appropriate for contacting an entity)
mechanics.save_dc: DC 15 Intelligence (Appropriate DC for a major, high-risk divination)
mechanics.condition_on_fail: Insane until long rest (Severe mechanical impact: no actions, no comprehension, gibberish speech; curable by _greater restoration_).
mechanics.success_benefit: Up to five questions answered by the entity (GM limits answers to one word or short phrase if necessary to avoid misleading the caster).

analysis.data_archivist_review: The data for 'Contact Other Plane' is complete, internally consistent, and mechanically sound based on established TTRPG design principles for high-level risk/reward spells. No errors found.
